patch.value:
GitHub: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/444

## Problem
Mutations fall back to author "unknown" when PM_AUTHOR is unset and author_default is empty (the init default). Stateless CLI agents get a fresh env per shell call, so attribution silently degrades to "unknown" — breaking history/claims/comments provenance that multi-agent coordination depends on.

## Proposed fix
(1) top-level global `--author <id>` flag; (2) `pm init` records a stable author_default (prompt or derive from git config/hostname); (3) pm health/validate warning when recent mutations carry author "unknown" with fix-hint `pm config set author-default <id>`.

## Definition of done
Global --author works on all mutations; init writes a non-empty author_default; unknown-author advisory ships with remediation-registry entry; docs/AGENTS.md updated. Related: GH-458 item covers the claim-time --assignee alias.
GH-444: ergonomic author attribution — global --author, init author_default, unknown-author advisory

Dogfood GH-444: stateless CLI agents lose PM_AUTHOR between shell calls and mutations degrade to author 'unknown', breaking provenance. Duplicate check: searched author attribution/unknown fallback — only closed dedup refactor pm-xh0y; no open overlap.
Global --author flag works on all mutations; pm init records non-empty author_default; health/validate advisory flags recent author:unknown mutations with fix-hint; docs updated
